# REGIME READ — 2026-06-12 (PM Update)

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage:** 5 → **3** (skipped Stage 4; the Iran-ceasefire announcement + VIX sub-20 crush + broad rally compressed the regime-change leg into a single session).
- **Total score:** -9 → **+1** (+10 — the largest single-read swing in this series, driven by the geopolitical flip and a parallel breadth/credit/vol unwind).
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — Verified Iran ceasefire / VXN crush / 50DMA reclaim:** **FIRED** (mostly). Trump announced a 60-day ceasefire framework Jun 11 with Hormuz reopening + Iranian mine-clearing + US sanctions waivers (Netanyahu publicly distanced Israel — partial caveat). VIX 22.22 → **19.44** (-12.51% Jun 11). TQQQ closed $76.01 vs 50DMA $76.39 (sitting on it Jun 11), and **Jun 12 intraday $77.47 is now decisively above the 50DMA**.
  - **Bullish — VIX sub-18:** ARMED, **NOT yet FIRED** (19.44 close).
  - **Bearish — TQQQ daily close below 200DMA $68.09:** **CLEARED.** TQQQ rallied +9.73% Jun 11; Jun 12 intraday $69.59-$77.07 range with last print $77.47 — the 200DMA test failed in the bullish direction.
  - **Bearish — VXN sustained > 28 / 32:** CLEARED with margin.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS > 320-400 bp:** ARMED, **moving away** (HY OAS ~275-276 bp, marginally tighter than prior read).
  - **Bearish — Hormuz fully shut:** ARMED. Framework now calls for reopening + mine-clearing, but physical reopening unverified; Netanyahu's distancing keeps some headline risk live.
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer Flow -1 → **+1** (vol crush continued + sub-20 VIX). Breadth -1 → **+1** (Dow +1.85%, SPX +1.75%, Nasdaq +2.54% Jun 11 = broad participation). Cross-Asset -1 → **+1** (oil rolling on ceasefire, HY tighter, gold rebounded but no panic). Geopolitical -2 → **+1** (60-day ceasefire framework — biggest single-lens flip). Positioning -1 → **0** (F&G 27.57 → 37 — still Fear, improving). Sentiment -1 → **0** (same). Macro held at **-2** (PPI shock).

> Methodology note: VXN specific Jun 11/12 print could not be retrieved from public sources at fetch time. Estimate (~18-19) inferred from VIX 19.44 close and typical VIX/VXN spread in a compressing-vol regime. Treat as estimate, not quote.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 3 — Neutral / choppy** (VIX 15-22, range-bound; risk-positive backdrop materially restored on Iran headline, but hot PPI and FOMC dot-plot tail keep risk environment from re-rating higher).
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 11, 2026 — Iran 60-day ceasefire announcement + VIX sub-20 close + broad +1.75% SPX rally.
- **Position:** Day 1-2 of new regime; structural durability untested.
- **Confidence: Medium** (5 of 7 lenses moved bullish in one session — that's a lot of correlation in a short window; FOMC Jun 17 and PPI follow-through are the verification tests).
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2):** VIX sustained sub-18 AND TQQQ daily close above pre-drop swing (~$80) AND FOMC Jun 17 either dovish-or-as-expected (no upward dot-plot revision for 2026), OR Hormuz physically reopened with verified mine-clearing.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4/5):** FOMC hawkish surprise (median 2026 dot raised), OR Iran framework breaks down within 14 days (Netanyahu/Israel re-engages), OR HY OAS through 320 bp, OR Brent re-spikes above $100, OR VIX back above 24 sustained.

## Regime Verdict
U.S. risk environment has flipped from **Stage 5 risk-off to Stage 3 neutral** in one session via the Iran ceasefire framework + parallel vol/credit/breadth unwind, with **Medium** confidence. The tape now reflects a removed-but-not-resolved war tail and an unresolved-and-fresh inflation tail; FOMC Jun 17 is the decision point.

## Seven-Lens Scorecard
| Lens | Score | Direction | Key Data (with dates) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Positioning | **-1 → 0** | Neutral (improving) | CNN F&G 37 Fear (Jun 11) up from 27.57; ETF flow detail / COT / NAAIM / AAII not retrievable |
| 2. Dealer / Options Flow | **-1 → +1** | Bullish | VIX 19.44 (Jun 11) -12.51%; VXN print unretrieved but parallel crush implied; term-structure not retrieved |
| 3. Macro / Liquidity | **-2 → -2** | Bearish (hot) | **PPI 6.5% YoY (May, released Jun 11) — biggest 12-mo rise since Nov 2022**; PPI final-demand +1.1% MoM SA; final-demand goods +2.8% MoM (largest since 2009); 10Y 4.55%→4.47%; 2s10s ~+40 bp normal-shape; HY OAS 275-276 bp tight; DXY ~100; Fed 98.2% hold odds at Jun 17 |
| 4. Breadth / Internals | **-1 → +1** | Bullish | Jun 11 rally was broad: Dow +1.85%, SPX +1.75%, Nasdaq Composite +2.54%; participation across cyclicals confirmed; specific % above 50DMA Jun 11 not retrieved |
| 5. Cross-Asset | **-1 → +1** | Bullish | Brent ~$88 / WTI $84.35 (Jun 12, -3.83%) on ceasefire = supply-shock unwind; Gold rebounded to $4,221-4,226 (Jun 12) — no panic, no flush; HY OAS ~275 bp tighter; DXY firm — risk-on signature |
| 6. Sentiment | **-1 → 0** | Neutral (improving) | F&G 27.57 → 37 Fear (Jun 11); sentiment lagging price (typical at regime turns) |
| 7. Geopolitical | **-2 → +1** | Bullish | **Trump 60-day ceasefire framework (Jun 11) with Hormuz reopening + Iranian mine-clearing + US sanctions waivers**; Netanyahu says "Israel not party" — risk premium NOT fully removed (-1 partial offset baked in) |
| **TOTAL** | **-9 → +1** | — | Risk-supportive on net; macro alone keeps the score from running higher |

## Diverging Signals
- **Macro lens is alone, but it's the loudest.** Six lenses moved bullish; one (Macro) deepened bearish. PPI 6.5% YoY (worst since Nov 2022) directly threatens any FOMC dovishness and is unresolved. The market is pricing the ceasefire and ignoring the inflation print — that's a coherent reaction only if you assume the Fed prioritizes growth/geopolitics over the print. Pre-FOMC, that's a bet, not a fact.
- **Adobe beat-and-fell.** ADBE Q2 beat EPS ($5.96 vs $5.94), revenue beat, AI-ARR tripled to >$500M, raised FY guidance — and the stock declined. In a risk-on tape, post-earnings strength normally extends. This is the "tech-leadership-broken" theme not yet fully repaired even as the index rallies. Worth watching whether tech leadership confirms or lags into next week.
- **Netanyahu vs. Trump on Iran framework.** Trump announced; Netanyahu publicly distanced Israel from the emerging deal Jun 11. The framework requires multi-party execution. Markets are treating it as priced; the actual Hormuz reopening / mine-clearing / verification is a sequence of operational events that can break down.
- **Vol crushed faster than fundamentals resolved.** VIX 32 (intraday Jun 10) → 19.44 (Jun 11) is a 39% crush in two sessions. Term-structure data not retrieved, but this kind of crush in front of a binary FOMC + hot PPI tends to be partially mechanical (pre-event positioning unwind) rather than fully fundamental. Premium has compressed faster than the FOMC tail has resolved.
- **WTI -3.83% on Jun 12 while Hormuz is still not physically reopen.** Oil is pricing the framework as if execution is certain. If Iran mine-clearing stalls or Israel re-engages, oil snap-back is the most asymmetric move in the tape.

## -- TQQQ INSTRUMENT ANALYSIS --

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI(14) **69.04 — near overbought**, sitting just below the 70 OB line after a +9.73% one-day move. Premium implication: **less rich than 24 hours ago**, and any further upside is now buying into momentum exhaustion territory (reversion risk grows above 70).
- **Market structure:** **SIDEWAYS.** Price $77.47 is **-12.0% from 4-wk high $88.09** (above the -20% DOWNTURN threshold) and **+13.8% from 4-wk low $68.08** (below the +30% RALLY threshold). DOWNTURN has cleared; RALLY has not been confirmed. The structural designation is SIDEWAYS, which is a regime-transitional read.
- **Trend:** Price is **above 50DMA** ($76.39) by 1.4% — narrow reclaim, not yet confirmed by multi-day hold. **Comfortably above 200DMA** ($68.09) by 13.8%. The two-MA test that was live Jun 10-11 has resolved bullish for now; durability requires a daily close hold above $76.39.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** VXN (estimated ~18-19) materially below the 23.27 prior-read level. TQQQ IV estimate ~55-57% (down from ~70%). Premium environment is **markedly thinner**. Gap-down distribution is narrower but FOMC binary remains.
- **Put-selling context:** **CAUTIOUS.** Three reasons. (1) The biggest tail (Iran/Hormuz war premium) has been removed in framework form but **not in execution form** — Netanyahu's distancing and unverified mine-clearing keep operational headline risk live for the next 14 days. (2) **FOMC Jun 16-17 sits inside the next 5 trading sessions** — first meeting for Chair Warsh, dot-plot release, against a fresh PPI 6.5% YoY print. The asymmetry favors hawkish surprise risk (median 2026 dot lift) over dovish, since Fed dovishness directly contradicts the just-released inflation data. (3) The IV crush from ~70% to ~55-57% means the reader is now being offered **materially less premium** for the **same FOMC tail risk**. The previous read's classic "post-capitulation + vol-still-elevated + base forming" setup has progressed — base is forming — but the vol leg has compressed faster than the FOMC binary has resolved. Counterpoint: the regime backdrop is genuinely better (geopolitical, breadth, credit, cross-asset all confirming), so the *qualitative* environment is more favourable than 48 hours ago even as the *quantitative* premium is thinner. The verdict is CAUTIOUS, not HOSTILE — the difference is whether you're being paid for what's left of the tail.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger:** TQQQ daily close above pre-drop swing area ~$80 AND VIX sustained sub-18, OR FOMC Jun 17 dot-plot held flat or revised dovish for 2026, OR Hormuz physically reopened with verified Iranian mine-clearing inside 14 days.
- **Bearish trigger:** FOMC Jun 17 hawkish surprise (median 2026 dot raised), OR TQQQ daily close back below 50DMA $76.39 AND VIX back above 22, OR Netanyahu/Israel re-engagement breaking the framework, OR Brent re-spike above $100, OR HY OAS through 300 bp.
- **Neutralizer:** A second hot inflation print (CPI Jul 10 area) that breaks the "Fed prioritizes growth over inflation" narrative, OR a stalled-execution headline on Hormuz reopening within 14 days.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 12, 2026 (today):** UMich Consumer Sentiment (preliminary); SpaceX market debut (largest IPO in history per reporting); Adobe Q2 post-earnings tape continues (beat but sold off).
- **Jun 15, 2026 (Mon):** NY Empire State manufacturing; Iran/Hormuz framework execution headlines.
- **Jun 16, 2026 (Tue):** **FOMC Day 1**; Retail Sales May; Industrial Production.
- **Jun 17, 2026 (Wed):** **FOMC decision + SEP / dot plot + Chair Warsh's first press conference**. 98.2% hold priced; the move is in the dots and the tone, not the rate.
- **Jun 18, 2026 (Thu):** Initial Jobless Claims; Philly Fed; first post-FOMC tape.
- **Ongoing:** Iran ceasefire framework execution (Hormuz mine-clearing, sanctions waivers, Israeli stance).

## Expiry-Window Flag
- Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 15 Mon, Jun 16 Tue, Jun 17 Wed, Jun 18 Thu, Jun 19 Fri): **FOMC decision Jun 17** (rate + SEP + dot plot + first-Warsh presser), Retail Sales Jun 16, ongoing Iran/Hormuz execution risk.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — a major central-bank decision falls inside the next 5 sessions, AND it is the first meeting for a new Fed Chair, AND it follows a hot PPI print.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook

- **FOMC Jun 16-17, 2026 (first meeting for Chair Warsh, post-hot-PPI hold, post-Iran-ceasefire rally).** This is an unusually loaded setup with no perfect comparable. Three named historical anchors:
  - **FOMC Jun 14-15, 2022 (CPI-shock regime, Powell):** SPX gapped lower into the meeting (-8% over two pre-meeting weeks), Fed delivered a hawkish 75bp surprise + raised dots, SPX bounced +1.5% intraday on the presser then **fell -5.8% over the next two weeks**. VIX entered at ~32, crushed to ~28 same-day, **re-spiked above 31 within a week**. The lesson: in a hot-inflation regime, even a "priced" rate decision can re-price the curve via dots and tone, unwinding any pre-meeting bounce.
  - **FOMC Sep 20-21, 2022 (post-hot CPI Sep 13):** SPX entered weak, hawkish dot-plot drove SPX -1.7% on the day and -5% over the following week into October 2022 capitulation. VIX 27 → 31. Pattern: **hot-PPI/CPI-into-FOMC has historically meant upward dot revisions and continued equity downside.**
  - **FOMC May 4, 2022 (first sustained-hike cycle of the cycle, Powell):** Powell ruled out 75bp at press conference — initial dovish read drove SPX +2.99% on the day, **fully reversed -3.56% the following day** as markets re-read the SEP. The lesson for a *first-Warsh-meeting* scenario: **markets often misread first-press-conference tone; the durable read takes 24-72 hours.**
  - **First-meeting-for-new-Chair precedents:** Powell's first FOMC (Mar 2018): rate hike + SPX -0.18% on the day, then -1.42% the following session as the dot-plot read settled. Yellen's first FOMC (Mar 2014): "six months after" tape-bomb on guidance, SPX -0.61% on the day. The historical pattern around new-Chair first-meetings is **lower confidence in the same-day tape and elevated reversal risk in the following 1-2 sessions**.
  - **VIX/VXN behavior:** Pre-FOMC vol-crush is typical (2-4 VIX points morning-of), then conditional: dovish → another 2-4 crush; hawkish → re-spike to or above pre-crush level within 48 hours. The 2022 episodes saw pre-meeting crushes fully unwind on hawkish prints; the current setup has *already* delivered most of the pre-meeting crush (VIX 32 → 19.44 over 48 hours), leaving less room for further dovish-on-decision compression and more room for hawkish-on-decision re-spike.
- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ THROUGH this FOMC means accepting a known-but-unquantifiable asymmetric tail under three compounding conditions: (1) hot PPI directly contradicts dovish-dot rationale, (2) first-Warsh meeting historically produces 24-72hr reversal risk on tone misreads, and (3) the pre-FOMC vol crush is **already substantially in price** (VIX 32 → 19.44), leaving more upside than downside in VIX into the print. Historical hot-CPI/PPI-into-FOMC episodes (Jun 2022, Sep 2022) saw 5-8% follow-through equity downside in the following 2 weeks. Counter-anchor: if Warsh frames the new dot-plot as *patient hold* rather than *higher-for-longer*, this is a 2024-Mar-style snap-squeeze setup. Gap risk is asymmetric to the downside; mean-reversion if dovish would be sharp but short-lived given near-OB RSI 69.04. **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - Analysis only. No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - VXN specific Jun 11/12 print, TQQQ exact Greeks, put/call ratio, term-structure prints (VIX9D/VIX3M, SKEW, VVIX), MOVE index, S&P 500 % above 50DMA Jun 11, NAAIM/AAII/COT detail are unavailable from public sources and marked accordingly.
> - VIX/TQQQ IV/EM ranges are clearly labeled as estimates (the IV input is itself estimated from VXN).
> - Conservative scoring applied: Positioning and Sentiment held at 0 (not +1) even though F&G improved, since the level remains in the Fear zone. Geopolitical at +1 (not +2) because Netanyahu publicly distanced Israel and physical Hormuz reopening is unverified.
> - The Stage 5 → Stage 3 transition skips Stage 4 — flagged as unusual and confidence held at Medium accordingly. FOMC Jun 17 is the verification event for whether Stage 3 holds or reverts.
